author: Bryan Guffey
message:
perf(sweep): parallelize second-degree follower fetch (#178)

The second-degree sweep awaited fetch_followers one first-degree
follower at a time. On a big-account onboarding this serial network
chain is the dominant cost — the measured #207 baseline was ~2h08m
pre-burst, ~100% Bluesky I/O — even though the classifier burst is
already solved.

Replace the serial for-loop with a buffer_unordered fan-out (capped at
concurrency.clamp(1,64), matching run_gather), then fold the results
sequentially. Dedup must stay sequential (a HashSet cannot be shared
across the in-flight futures) but it is cheap; all the wall-clock was in
the awaits. The fold is extracted into a pure dedup_second_degree helper
so its order-independence — the one real risk of going concurrent, since
buffer_unordered delivers batches in arbitrary order — is unit-tested.

The produced second-degree pool is the same set as before: a follower's
profile fields are its own, so which first-degree follower we discover
them through is immaterial.

Tests: 2 new unit tests (order-independence + error-skip); full web
suite (31 binaries) green; clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BYxHfK818WVCcq5oEX3S12

fix(cope-b): lower classify-gate agreement bar 0.90->0.85

The live RunPod CoPE-B gate plateaued at ~89% agreement against Zentropi.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
perf(cope-b): cut full-cold-start time (prefetch weights, persist compile cache)

Cold-start log review (chainlink #201). FlashBoot already keeps the
steady-state cold start at ~14s (P70-P98); these target the rare full
cold start (~285s): 208s weight load, 30.6s torch.compile, ~21s
cudagraph/KV/warmup, ~25s container.

- safetensors_load_strategy="prefetch": the RunPod network volume mounts
  as FUSE, which vLLM does not treat as a network FS, so it disables
  auto-prefetch and reads the 11 shards serially (~25-29s each). Prefetch
  overlaps the shard reads.
- VLLM_CACHE_ROOT=/runpod-volume/vllm-cache: persist torch.compiles cache
  on the volume so later cold starts skip the ~30s recompile.
- RUNPOD_INIT_TIMEOUT=800: raise the worker-init health cutoff above the
  default 7-min mark for margin.

Config/engine-arg only; handler tests mock the engine. Validated at the
next GPU cold start.

feat(cope-b): identity hate overrides the third-party pass in policy

The prior revision made the third-party exclusion so forceful that CoPE-B
started clearing identity slurs framed as group statements — "all trannies
are groomers and pedos" flipped to a false negative (Zentropi correctly
flags it). Carve identity-based hate out of the third-party rule:

- Identity-attack rule now explicitly covers slurs / dehumanizing /
  criminalizing claims against a protected class even when aimed at the
  whole group rather than a named participant.
- Third-party pass gets an EXCEPTION: it does not cover identity hate.
- Decisive-defaults closing line excepts identity hate from the
  "third-party -> 0" route.

Targeted follow-up to the 88% gate run (which fixed political third-party
FPs but introduced this identity FN). Bryan-gated: re-gate once after this.

Chainlink #189.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix(cope-b): poll /status when /runsync returns before completion

RunPods /runsync waits ~90s then returns the current job envelope; for a
job that has not finished (cold starts routinely exceed that), the body is
{"id","status":"IN_PROGRESS"} with NO output. RunPodCopeBClient assumed
the verdict was always present, so the first live gate call failed with
"missing field output" — and every production cold start would too.

Parse the full job envelope (status + optional output) via parse_job ->
JobOutcome::{Completed,Pending}. On Pending, poll /status/{id} every 2s
until terminal or the classifier timeout elapses. 5xx while polling is
treated as transient; FAILED/CANCELLED/TIMED_OUT surface the error.

Adds a wiremock regression test: /runsync IN_PROGRESS -> /status COMPLETED.

Chainlink #200.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix(cope-b): reset base ENTRYPOINT so the RunPod handler actually runs

The vllm/vllm-openai base image sets its own ENTRYPOINT (the vLLM OpenAI
API server). Our Dockerfile only set CMD, so at runtime the container ran
the vLLM server with "python3 -u handler.py" appended as args — handler.py
never executed, runpod.serverless.start never registered a job-taker, and
every request sat IN_QUEUE while workers idled/cycled and exited.

Add `ENTRYPOINT []` to clear the inherited entrypoint so our CMD runs the
handler as the container main process. (Caught at first live deploy; this
is the failure the deferred GPU smoke test would have surfaced.)

Chainlink #200.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat(cope-b): serve weights from a RunPod network volume, not baked image

The CoPE-B-A4B model is ~50GB (Gemma-4 26B-A4B, 25B params). Baking it
into the image produced a ~60GB image that cannot build on GitHub-hosted
runners (runner dies on disk) and re-pulls on every cold start. Move the
weights to a RunPod network volume mounted at /runpod-volume, populated
once and reused across cold starts — preserving the spec download-once
goal without the giant image.

Dockerfile: drop the huggingface-cli weight-download layer and the
MODEL_REVISION arg; image is now just the vLLM base + app code.
MODEL_PATH defaults to /runpod-volume/cope-b-a4b.
runpod.yml: add network_volume (charcoal-cope-b-weights, 70GB, mounted at
/runpod-volume) + env MODEL_PATH; endpoint and volume must share a DC.
workflow: remove the free-disk-space step and MODEL_REVISION build-arg;
restore GHA layer caching (layers are small now).
README: document the create-volume / populate-volume / create-endpoint
order of operations.

handler.py is unchanged — it already reads MODEL_PATH from env, so a
local volume path works as-is.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix(cope-b): address code review on Chunk 3 RunPod GPU service

Fix 1 (Important): reorder Dockerfile layers so the ~50 GB weight-download
RUN runs BEFORE copying app code. Its cache key now depends only on
MODEL_REVISION, so a policy.txt-only edit rebuilds just the final tiny app
COPY instead of invalidating the weight layer. Fixed the false comment in
build-cope-b-image.yml that described the old (broken) ordering.

Fix 2 (Minor): prefix-cache benchmark now builds its prompt from the
production prompt.build_body() and posts it to /v1/chat/completions, so
vLLM applies the Gemma chat template server-side. This validates the exact
production prefix without needing a local tokenizer (server owns the
template). Previously it posted a hand-rolled string to /v1/completions.

Fix 3 (Minor): build_body now splits the static template on its sentinels
once at import and joins the fixed segments with the policy/content values,
so a literal __POLICY__/__CONTENT__ inside user/policy text is never
re-substituted. Output is byte-identical for normal inputs. Added
test_build_body_not_corrupted_by_literal_sentinel_in_policy.

Fix 4 (Nit): documented that _get_engine() check-and-set is race-safe only
because _build_engine() is synchronous (no await between check and assign);
an async _build_engine would need an asyncio.Lock.

Runnable tests: 9 passed, 5 skipped (skips need transformers / live vLLM).

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat(tools): add discover mode to find-fixtures

discover mode enumerates lists owned by one or more known maintainers
and emits a catalog (TSV by default, JSONL with --json) the reviewer
can grep/sort to pick which list URI to feed into list mode.

--match filters list name + description; --purpose filters by
modlist/curatelist/referencelist/all (default modlist); --min-members
skips empty test lists.

Honest scope note in the help text and README: the public AT Protocol
API has NO global searchLists endpoint, so discovery is graph-walk
(seed with maintainers you trust). When zero lists are returned, the
tip message points the user back at the seed-maintainer pattern rather
than suggesting a global search is possible.

Smoke verified against pfrazee.com + why.bsky.team — returned the
expected 3 visible lists in TSV; --match filter correctly returned 0
when nothing matched.

Chainlink #186.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat(tools): add list mode + --match regex filter to find-fixtures

list mode walks every member of a Bluesky moderation/curation list and
harvests their authored posts via getAuthorFeed. This is the
highest-signal candidate source because mod-list maintainers have
already done the per-account curation we would otherwise have to guess
at, and the list usually carries a documented theme (transphobia,
antisemitism, specific harassment crew) so members share a language
pattern.

--match <regex> filter wired through all three modes (author / backlinks
/ list). Answers the "what words should we search for" question by
letting you iterate hand-tuned regexes rather than freeform searching —
which the public AppView gates behind auth anyway.

Block-resilience note: many hostile accounts likely have already blocked
the protected user, so they wont appear in Constellation backlinks of
the protected users posts. They DO appear on mod lists and getAuthorFeed
works regardless of who they have blocked (we query as anonymous
AppView).

bsky_url_to_at_uri generalized to parse both /post/<rkey> and
/lists/<rkey> URLs. list mode tags each row with note=from-list-member:<did>
so the reviewer can trace candidates back to their source.

Verified:
  - author + match against chaosgreml.in returned 4 trans/queer-mentioning
    candidates with envelope shape correct
  - list mode plumbing tested against a real (empty) public list owned by
    pfrazee.com; 0 emitted is the correct behavior for an empty list

Chainlink #186.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix: address PR #46 round-3 CodeRabbit findings

Four findings from the third review pass. All real, all fixed.

Fixed:

- topic_search.rs (discover_by_topic error swallowing): the loop over
  keywords previously logged each `search_posts_for_authors` failure
  via `tracing::warn!` and continued. If every keyword search failed
  (e.g., AT Proto outage), the function returned `Ok(vec![])` —
  indistinguishable from a clean topic neighborhood with no matches.
  Now tracks a `successful_searches` counter and a `last_error` chain;
  if the keyword list was non-empty and zero searches succeeded, returns
  an `Err` so callers can react to the outage. Single-keyword failures
  still fall through (best-effort across the cycle).

- profile.rs (Stage 1 vacuous-pass guard): the round-2 fix excluded
  reply scores from `stage1_clean_pass_scores`, but a reply-only or
  reply-heavy sample (e.g., 1 original + 24 replies) could still pass
  vacuously because `iter().all()` returns true for empty slices and
  for tiny "all 1 entry below threshold" slices that didn't actually
  inspect the bulk of the content. Added
  `MIN_FIRST_PERSON_POSTS_FOR_EARLY_EXIT = 5` constant and a guard in
  `should_early_exit_stage1`: samples with fewer than 5 originals+quotes
  always proceed to Stage 2, where Zentropi can do pair classification
  with parent context. New tests pin both the empty-slice and the
  too-few-entries cases.

- profile.rs (graph_distance dropped on early returns): the
  "Insufficient Data" early return and the Stage 1 early-exit return
  both set `graph_distance: None`, throwing away the value the caller
  had computed. `GraphDistance` is `Copy` so the same value is reachable
  from all three return sites; both early returns now set
  `graph_distance: graph_distance.map(|d| d.as_str().to_string())`,
  matching the full-pipeline branch. Downstream consumers (sweep
  ranking, UI) preserve the signal even on insufficient-data and
  early-exit accounts.

- zentropi.rs (module-doc clarity): the doc comment didn't make clear
  that `refs/labeler_prompt.txt` is a checked-in reference snapshot
  rather than the runtime source. Added an explicit "Policy source of
  truth" section: the active policy lives on Zentropi's side,
  identified by `ZENTROPI_LABELER_ID` and pinned by
  `ZENTROPI_LABELER_VERSION_ID`; runtime behavior changes only after
  re-uploading the policy and bumping the version env var. Maintainers
  who edit the reference file should now know it has no runtime effect
  on its own.

Tests: 419 passing (was 417 — gained 2 new tests for the early-exit
guards). Clippy `-D warnings` clean across `--features web`,
`--features postgres`, and no-features. Rustfmt clean.

Refs: #173, #175

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phase 5 — Zentropi binary classifier replaces Groq ensemble

ONNX becomes a clean-pass filter (< 0.10 = cleared); posts at or above
the threshold go to Zentropi for a binary toxic/safe verdict that drives
the threat formula's toxicity rate. Replaces the ONNX+Groq Safeguard
ensemble approach with a two-stage pipeline matching the architecture
briefing's target state.

Why: Phase 0 spike (#173) validated Zentropi at 100% accuracy / 190ms
latency. Groq Safeguard was an unsustainable-cost stopgap; Zentropi's
conversation-scoped labeler policy correctly distinguishes ally use of
identity terms from hostile use, which the ONNX model cannot.

Toxicity scoring:
- New `src/toxicity/zentropi.rs` — production `ZentropiClient` with
  10s timeout, 3-retry exponential backoff on 5xx/429/network errors,
  and a `run_diagnostic` smoke-test method.
- New `TwoStageToxicityScorer` (in `ensemble.rs`) wraps a continuous
  primary (ONNX or Perspective) with an optional Zentropi binary
  classifier. Bounded parallelism via `buffer_unordered(4)`. Falls back
  to ONNX 0.50 threshold when Zentropi is unavailable so local dev
  still works without a key.
- Added `BinaryVerdict` + `classify_batch_with_contexts` to the
  `ToxicityScorer` trait. Default impl uses a 0.50 threshold;
  `TwoStageToxicityScorer` overrides with the real two-stage pipeline.

Scoring formula:
- `build_profile` now batch-classifies with per-post parent context for
  replies (so the Zentropi labeler evaluates reply-toward-author
  hostility correctly).
- Toxicity rate = reply-weighted binary count via the existing
  `compute_reply_weighted_toxicity`. Replies count 70%, originals +
  quotes count 30%.
- Evidence ranking: surface Zentropi-flagged posts first, sorted by
  ONNX score; fall back to highest-ONNX when nothing is flagged.
- `weighted_toxicity` (the ONNX-category-weighted patch for ONNX's
  inability to distinguish intent) removed from the scoring path.

Wiring:
- `src/main.rs` and `src/web/scan_job.rs` build `TwoStageToxicityScorer`
  from a new `build_zentropi[_client]` helper. `Commands::ZentropiSpike`
  renamed to `Commands::ZentropiCheck` with neutral OK/WARN messaging
  (the spike was the validation; this is now the smoke test).

Removed:
- `src/toxicity/groq_safeguard.rs` and `groq_api_key` config field.
- `src/toxicity/zentropi_spike.rs` (promoted to `zentropi.rs`).
- `docs/superpowers/{plans,specs}/2026-03-27-groq-ensemble-scorer-*.md`.

Tests: 416 passing (was 410 — gained 12 new two-stage tests, lost the
6 Groq parsing tests). Clippy `-D warnings` clean across `--features
web`, `--features postgres`, and no-features. Rustfmt clean.

Refs: #173

author: chaos gremlin
message:
feat: multi-user schema redesign (v0.5) (#14)

* docs: add multi-user schema redesign implementation plan

11-task plan covering schema migration v4, Database trait changes,
both backend implementations, pipeline threading, CLI DID resolution,
web handler auth extraction, and test updates.

* feat(db): add migration v4 — multi-user schema with user_did columns

Creates users table, rebuilds topic_fingerprint/account_scores/scan_state
with user_did in their primary keys, adds user_did column to
amplification_events, and rebuilds indices to include user_did.
Existing data backfilled with empty string user_did.

* feat(db): add user_did parameter to all Database trait methods

Add user_did as first parameter to every user-scoped method in the
Database trait, enabling per-user data isolation. Also add upsert_user
method for user management. table_count remains global (unscoped).

Implementations will be updated in subsequent tasks.

* feat(db): scope all SQLite queries to user_did

All 17 query functions now take user_did as a parameter to scope
data per protected user. SQL queries updated with composite key
WHERE clauses and INSERT columns. Added upsert_user() function
for the new users table. All tests updated with TEST_USER constant
and user isolation tests added.

* feat(db): update SqliteDatabase trait impl for user_did params

Forward user_did to all queries::* calls, add upsert_user method,
update all tests with TEST_USER constant, fix table_count assertion
for migration v4 (5 -> 6 tables).

* feat(db): update PgDatabase for multi-user schema

Add migration v4 (0004_multiuser.sql) that creates users table and adds
user_did column to all data tables with updated composite primary keys.

Update all PgDatabase trait method implementations to accept user_did
parameter and scope all queries per-user. Add upsert_user method.

* feat(pipeline): thread user_did through amplification, sweep, and status

* feat(cli): resolve handle to DID and thread user_did through all commands

Every CLI command that calls DB methods now:
1. Resolves config.bluesky_handle to a DID
2. Calls db.upsert_user() to ensure the user row exists
3. Passes user_did to all DB and pipeline function calls

Added resolve_and_register_user() helper. Updated load_fingerprint()
and load_embedder() to accept user_did parameter. Status command
gracefully handles missing BLUESKY_HANDLE with empty string fallback.
Migrate command registers user in both source and target databases.

* feat(web): extract user_did from auth context in all handlers

All protected web handlers now extract AuthUser from request
extensions and pass user_did to Database trait methods. The
scan_job launch_scan/run_scan functions accept user_did and
thread it through fingerprint, embedding, engagement, pile-on,
and pipeline calls.

* test: update integration tests for multi-user schema

All DB-touching integration tests now pass user_did to trait methods.
PostgreSQL tests use TEST_USER constant for user-scoped operations.

* docs: update CLAUDE.md for multi-user schema (v4)

Add multi-user schema bullet to post-MVP improvements. Update test
count to 225, Database trait method count to 18, schema version to v4.

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings on PR #14

- Wrap SQLite v4 migration in BEGIN/COMMIT for atomicity
- Drop DEFAULT on user_did columns in Postgres migration after backfill
- Seed sweep dedupe set with user_did (DID) instead of protected_handle
- Show last_scan_at independently of notifications_cursor in status
- Return 500 errors instead of unwrap_or_default in accounts/events handlers
- Wrap background scan in catch_unwind to reset status on panic
- Append UTC offset to detected_at in Postgres event insertion
- Log warning on DID resolution failure in status command
- Scope Postgres test cleanup by user_did for amplification_events

* fix: address second round of CodeRabbit review findings on PR #14

- Fix negative UTC offset detection in Postgres event timestamp binding
- Use checked i32 cast for max_age_days in is_score_stale
- Rebuild amplification_events table in SQLite v4 migration (no DEFAULT on user_did)
- Propagate DB errors for median_engagement and pile_on in scan job

* fix: propagate DB errors from get_embedding in scan job

Previously mapped all Err to None, hiding real DB failures like
connection errors. Now Ok(None) still means "no embedding" (TF-IDF
fallback) while Err propagates and fails the scan.

* fix: thread authenticated actor handle through web scan job

The scan job was using config.bluesky_handle which could be empty or
belong to a different user in multi-user mode. Now launch_scan accepts
the actor_handle from the authenticated session, looked up via the new
get_user_handle trait method. Both fetch_recent_posts and the
amplification pipeline use the per-user handle instead of the global
config value.

author: musicjunkieg
message:
feat: v0.2 backend polish — close #68, #72, #73, #74, #75, #76

  #73 — Add PostgreSQL setup section to README documenting pgvector
    CREATE EXTENSION superuser requirement and migration command.

  #75 — Replace silent post_count as i32 cast with i32::try_from()
    in PgDatabase::save_fingerprint, surfacing an error instead of
    silently wrapping on overflow.

  #76 — Gate sqlite feature: make rusqlite optional (dep:rusqlite),
    add cfg(feature = "sqlite") to mod sqlite/schema/queries and
    all sqlite-specific functions in db/mod.rs.

  #74 — Add cleanup_test_data() helper to db_postgres.rs that deletes
    test-specific rows before each writing test so runs are idempotent.

  #72 — Acquire pg_advisory_lock(0x43484152434F414C) before running
    migrations and release it after, preventing race conditions when
    two processes start concurrently.

  #68 — Rewrite detect_pile_on_participants() as O(n) two-pointer
    sliding window. Previous implementation was O(n^2). New approach
    uses a HashMap<DID, count> maintained as left/right pointers
    advance monotonically, so each event enters and exits at most once.

  intelligence pipeline. All read operations now use the unauthenticated
  public API at public.api.bsky.app via a new PublicAtpClient (reqwest).

  scores blocked accounts via PDS repo access to verify pipeline accuracy.

Complete the core threat detection pipeline:

  and score each one
